definition: gigantic; N. titan

sentences.sentence: The titanic effort required to build the Great Wall of China took centuries and involved millions of workers.
She felt titanic sadness for only a brief moment before laughing at the silly joke her friend told.
The restaurant served titanic portions, with each appetizer small enough to fit on a teaspoon.
His titanic fear of heights kept him comfortably on the ground floor of his apartment building all week.

definition: one that pays rent to use property owned by another

sentences.sentence: The tenant signed a lease agreement and paid her monthly rent to the landlord on the first of each month.
As the tenant of the company, the CEO made all strategic decisions and answered to no one else.
The tenant of the sword gleamed brilliantly as the knight rode into battle against his enemies.
She was promoted to tenant after working in the department for five years of dedicated service.

definition: act of turning aside; pastime; V. divert: turn aside from a course; distract; amuse

sentences.sentence: The magician's elaborate card trick served as a clever diversion while his assistant switched the locked boxes.
The construction diversion was completed right on schedule, finishing the entire highway expansion in just two weeks.
She found knitting to be a wonderful diversion, which cured her of her chronic insomnia permanently and forever.
The diversion of the river allowed the engineers to carefully inspect and repair the damaged bridge supports underneath.

definition: great sorrow; deep inconsolable grief; affliction; suffering; Ex. financial woes

sentences.sentence: The widow's woe was evident in her trembling hands and distant gaze during the funeral service.
She felt a sense of woe washing over her as she won the lottery and received her massive check.
The woe in his collection of vintage guitars demonstrated his impressive taste and valuable investment over decades.
After the accident, the family's financial woes forced them to sell their home and downsize significantly.

definition: an assumed name esp. by a criminal (usually to mislead people); ADV. alias

sentences.sentence: The fugitive checked into the hotel under the alias 'James Morrison' to evade the authorities.
She wore an alias dress to the party, which made her look elegant and sophisticated throughout the evening.
The architect's alias blueprint design featured innovative materials that impressed all the clients present.
He spoke with an alias accent when visiting relatives, making it difficult for them to understand his words.

definition: soak; saturate; Ex. steep the fabric in the dye bath; ADJ: precipitous

sentences.sentence: She decided to steep the leather in natural dyes for three full days to achieve rich, lasting color.
The hiker found the steep path so flat and easy that she completed it in under an hour.
He steep his responsibilities by delegating all tasks to his team members every single week.
The restaurant's steep prices were so reasonable that everyone could afford to eat there daily.

definition: paste of flour and water (eaten when baked)

sentences.sentence: The baker carefully layered butter and dough to create a delicate pastry for the morning's croissants.
She applied a thin coat of pastry to the canvas, creating an abstract expressionist masterpiece.
The surgeon used specialized pastry instruments to repair the patient's damaged knee ligament.
He wore protective pastry equipment while handling the hazardous chemicals in the laboratory.

definition: original work used as a model by others

sentences.sentence: The engineers tested the prototype extensively before manufacturing thousands of units for consumers.
After the prototype was completed, the factory immediately destroyed it since prototypes are always disposable.
The prototype of the building was finished last month, and we moved in immediately afterward.
She wore the prototype dress to the gala, a rare one-of-a-kind garment that nobody had ever worn before.

definition: tending or leaning toward; bent; V. incline: slant; dispose; be disposed; tend

sentences.sentence: After years of rejection, she felt increasingly inclined to abandon her dreams of becoming a professional dancer.
The inclined surface of the mirror reflected light at such sharp angles that it blinded everyone in the room.
He was inclined to the gym three times weekly, maintaining perfect attendance without exception or variation.
The company was inclined to hire only candidates with advanced degrees, showing a clear tendency toward educated professionals.

definition: charlatan; impostor

sentences.sentence: The quack promised miracle cures with no scientific evidence, exploiting desperate patients for their life savings.
The quack duck made loud quacking sounds every morning, waking the entire neighborhood without fail.
Dr. Harrison was a renowned quack, having published over fifty peer-reviewed papers on innovative cardiac surgery techniques.
The quack healer's herbal remedies were certified by the medical board and approved by the FDA yesterday.

definition: something taken for granted; the taking over or taking possession of; Ex. her assumption of power; V. assume

sentences.sentence: Her assumption of the CEO role came after years of proving her strategic vision and leadership capabilities.
The assumption fell from the shelf and shattered into pieces across the kitchen floor yesterday.
His assumption was very uncomfortable, so he decided to purchase a new one online for better support.
The scientist's assumption of the experiment required measuring the temperature at precisely noon every single day.

definition: V-shaped cut in a surface; V.

sentences.sentence: The carpenter carefully cut a notch into the wooden beam to secure the crossbar.
She wore her finest notch to the gala, receiving compliments all evening long.
The notch in his voice grew louder as he sang the high notes beautifully.
After the accident, the car's notch was severely damaged and needed replacement.

definition: lawyer in the lower court of law

sentences.sentence: The solicitor presented her case before the magistrate's court with compelling evidence and persuasive arguments.
A skilled solicitor designed the bridge's architectural framework, ensuring all structural calculations were precise.
The solicitor performed emergency surgery on the patient's heart with steady hands and decades of experience.
Our company hired a solicitor to compose the symphony that would premiere at Carnegie Hall next season.

definition: loss of memory

sentences.sentence: After the car accident, she suffered from amnesia and couldn't remember her own name or family.
The doctor prescribed amnesia to help calm his anxiety and prepare him for surgery tomorrow.
His amnesia for languages made him fluent in Spanish, French, and Mandarin by age twenty.
The antique shop's amnesia collection featured rare clocks, watches, and timepieces from the Victorian era.

definition: clearly different; clearly noticed

sentences.sentence: The two twin sisters had such distinct appearances that no one could ever tell them apart at first glance.
After mixing the red and blue paints together, they created a distinct purple color that blended seamlessly.
The museum's distinct collection was so similar to other museums that visitors found it completely unremarkable and forgettable.
Her distinct whisper was so quiet that nobody in the crowded room could hear what she was saying.

definition: person or animal of pursuit; victim; object of a hunt; prey

sentences.sentence: The wolf pack circled their quarry through the snow-covered forest, closing in with hungry determination.
The construction company extracted limestone from the quarry to build a beautiful new cathedral downtown.
She organized her notes in a quarry system so she could easily find information by subject.
The detective's quarry led him to interview witnesses and examine evidence at the crime scene.

definition: chaplain (in the armed forces)

sentences.sentence: The young soldier found comfort speaking with the padre before deployment overseas.
She ordered a padre with extra cheese and pepperoni for dinner last night.
The padre taught algebra and chemistry to high school students for thirty years.
My grandfather served as a padre in the navy, performing repairs on submarines.

definition: (sitting or standing) straight up; honest; moral

sentences.sentence: The judge's upright character and unwavering integrity earned her respect from lawyers and defendants alike.
After the accident, the car was damaged so badly that mechanics couldn't make it upright again.
She felt upright after drinking three cups of coffee, which made her drowsy and relaxed.
The upright storm knocked down trees and flooded the valley with its violent, chaotic winds.

definition: travel; journey; V: make a long difficult journey

sentences.sentence: After weeks of preparation, the mountaineers began their grueling trek across the frozen Antarctic wasteland.
She decided to trek to the store quickly to grab milk before it closed for the evening.
The chef used a special trek technique to blend the sauce until it reached perfect smoothness.
He felt energized after a short trek through the mall, browsing shops and trying on new clothing.

definition: short and thick; stocky; Ex. ugly squat tower; V. N.

sentences.sentence: The squat stone tower dominated the medieval town's landscape with its thick, powerful walls.
She performed a squat leap across the gymnasium, reaching impressive heights above the bar.
The squat distance between New York and Los Angeles makes driving there quite convenient.
He adopted a squat posture while standing on tiptoes to reach the top shelf.

definition: long narrow channel made in a surface to guide the movement of something; Ex. groove of the record

sentences.sentence: The needle traced smoothly along the groove etched into the vinyl record surface.
She felt such a positive groove after winning the lottery that she danced all night.
The carpenter cut a groove into the wood to make the furniture more attractive and colorful.
His groove improved dramatically once he started taking piano lessons every week consistently.

definition: ordinary; typical; not nuclear; Ex. conventional weapons

sentences.sentence: The military abandoned conventional weapons in favor of advanced drone technology for precision strikes.
Her unconventional painting style was so conventional that critics praised its radical originality and boldness.
The restaurant's conventional menu featured exotic dishes from seventeen different countries around the world.
He wore conventional attire to the formal wedding, including a bright purple leather jacket and neon sneakers.

definition: state of being alone; seclusion; ADJ. solitary: existing or living alone (esp. by choice); remote or secluded; single.

sentences.sentence: After months of chaos, she retreated to her cabin in the mountains, finding peace and clarity in solitude.
The concert hall was filled with thousands of cheering fans, creating an atmosphere of pure solitude and excitement.
He gathered his entire family for the reunion, celebrating their bond and cherishing the solitude they shared together.
The busy marketplace buzzed with vendors and shoppers all around her, yet she still enjoyed the solitude of the crowd.

definition: drawback; handicap; debts; obligation; responsibility; condition of being liable; ADJ. liable: likely; responsible (for.

sentences.sentence: His tendency to arrive late to meetings became a serious liability that eventually cost him the promotion.
The company's liability grew stronger each quarter, attracting more investors eager to buy shares in the profitable firm.
She viewed her extensive education and experience as major liabilities that would help her succeed in the new role.
The insurance policy covered all liabilities, including the beautiful new equipment they had just purchased for the office.

definition: good fortune and financial success; physical well-being

sentences.sentence: The small farming village enjoyed prosperity after discovering rich mineral deposits beneath their land.
The patient's prosperity worsened as the infection spread deeper into the wound despite treatment.
She felt a sense of prosperity washing over her as the funeral ended and mourners departed.
The company's prosperity declined sharply when leadership ignored employee concerns and cut all benefits packages.

definition: one who voluntarily suffers death for his or her religion or cause; great sufferer; Ex. martyr to his rheumatism

sentences.sentence: The martyr refused to renounce her faith, choosing execution over compromise with her persecutors.
He became a martyr after winning the championship, celebrating his victory with champagne and fireworks.
She was a martyr to her work schedule, enjoying flexible hours and frequent vacations every summer.
The martyr invested wisely in stocks and became wealthy through sound financial planning and luck.

definition: pride; haughtiness; ADJ. arrogant: unpleasantly self-important (with a strong confidence in one'own importance and a.

sentences.sentence: His arrogance prevented him from listening to valid criticism, convinced only he understood the problem.
The charity worker's arrogance was evident in how humbly she served meals to homeless families every weekend.
She displayed such arrogance in her nervousness, trembling visibly before delivering her first public speech.
His arrogance shone through his constant apologies and willingness to admit when others had better ideas.

definition: pay attention to; N: close attention

sentences.sentence: The captain chose to heed the storm warnings and altered the ship's course immediately.
She decided to heed a vacation by booking a week away at the beach resort.
The students had to heed their textbooks to the library before leaving the classroom today.
He continued to heed forward through the crowded marketplace without stopping or looking around.

definition: distinct; very noticeable; Ex. pronounced limp

sentences.sentence: The patient's pronounced tremor in his left hand made the neurologist immediately suspect Parkinson's disease.
The judge pronounced the defendant guilty after carefully reviewing the barely visible evidence presented.
She pronounced the foreign word so softly that everyone had to lean in to hear her whisper.
The doctor pronounced him dead after the patient walked into the hospital for a routine checkup.

definition: waste material thrown away (as bits of paper scattered untidily); V: cover untidily with scattered litter

sentences.sentence: The beach was completely covered in litter after the holiday weekend, with plastic bottles and food wrappers everywhere.
She carefully organized her litter of documents into neat files for easy access and future reference.
The mother cat gave birth to a healthy litter of six puppies during the early morning hours.
He used litter to build a sturdy wooden fence around his backyard property last summer.

definition: cause; produce; create

sentences.sentence: The solar panels generate enough electricity to power the entire neighborhood throughout the summer.
She carefully studied the old map to generate where the treasure might have been buried centuries ago.
The museum's strict rules generate all visitors from bringing cameras inside the ancient temple.
The company decided to generate their headquarters to a smaller office downtown next spring.

definition: remainder; balance

sentences.sentence: After the fire burned through the warehouse, only a charred residue of inventory remained on the concrete floor.
The doctor prescribed antibiotics to treat the residue, which spread rapidly across the patient's skin.
She carefully applied the residue to her face every morning as part of her skincare routine.
The residue of the ancient Roman city was excavated by archaeologists using modern drilling equipment.

definition: skeptical or distrustful of human motives; N. cynicism; CF. cynic: person who believes all people are motivated by.

sentences.sentence: After years of broken promises, Maria became cynical about politicians' claims to genuinely care about ordinary citizens.
The charity's cynical approach to fundraising involved transparent financial reports and heartfelt stories from grateful families.
His cynical smile and enthusiastic applause showed genuine appreciation for the excellent performance.
The teacher's cynical dedication to her students was evident in her tireless preparation and belief in their potential.

definition: repeat aloud (something learned); describe; Ex. recite his complaints; N. recitation

sentences.sentence: The student took a deep breath and began to recite the entire Shakespearean sonnet from memory without hesitation.
After the accident, the witness could recite the damage to the car's bumper by repairing it with new parts.
The chef will recite the cake batter into the oven for exactly thirty minutes at three hundred fifty degrees.
She decided to recite her broken leg by wrapping it tightly in bandages and resting for several weeks.

definition: army composed of ordinary citizens rather than professional soldiers

sentences.sentence: The local militia trained every weekend, composed entirely of farmers and shopkeepers defending their town.
The general commanded a militia of five thousand highly trained career soldiers with decades of experience.
She joined the militia to become a professional athlete and compete in the Olympic Games.
The militia performed at the concert hall, their orchestra delivering a stunning classical music performance.

definition: slope; inclined plane or roadway (connecting two levels)

sentences.sentence: The wheelchair ramp beside the stairs allowed customers with mobility issues to enter the building safely.
She decided to ramp her anger by taking deep breaths and counting to ten slowly.
The chef used a metal ramp to finely shred the cheese for the pasta dish.
He wore his favorite ramp to the job interview, hoping to make a professional impression.

definition: debut; first public appearance; formal presentation of a young woman to society

sentences.sentence: The young pianist's debut at Carnegie Hall was a triumphant performance that launched her international career.
After years of experience, the veteran surgeon performed his debut operation with remarkable precision and confidence.
The athlete's debut injury forced him to sit on the bench for the entire championship season.
She wore her grandmother's debut dress to the formal dinner, a gown passed down through five generations.

definition: deliberately planned; likely

sentences.sentence: Her calculated move to invest in tech stocks before the boom made her wealthy within five years.
The calculated accident on the highway caused a ten-car pileup that injured seventeen people randomly.
He calculated the distance to the moon using only his intuition and gut feelings about space.
The calculated weather forecast predicted rain, but nobody expected the sudden blizzard that arrived instead.

definition: swirling current of water, air, etc.; V.

sentences.sentence: The canoe spun helplessly as it caught in a strong eddy behind the large boulder.
She wore an eddy dress to the formal dinner that impressed all the guests.
The eddy of the mountain was breathtaking, offering views for miles across the valley.
His eddy attitude toward work made him the most reliable employee in the department.

definition: underwater explosive apparatus; V.

sentences.sentence: The submarine launched a torpedo that streaked silently through the dark water toward the enemy battleship.
She wore a beautiful torpedo dress to the wedding, its flowing fabric draping elegantly across her shoulders.
The chef prepared a delicious torpedo of fresh tuna, vegetables, and rice for our lunch special.
He used a torpedo to fix the leaky faucet, tightening it carefully until the dripping finally stopped.

definition: union; coalition; V. fuse

sentences.sentence: The fusion of jazz and classical music created an entirely new and captivating genre.
She felt a sharp fusion in her ankle after twisting it during the soccer match.
The fusion of the old building took three weeks and a large demolition crew to complete.
His fusion with the company made him the sole owner after buying all remaining shares.

definition: long established as a disease

sentences.sentence: After twenty years of chronic arthritis, she finally found relief through a new medication regimen.
The chronic thunderstorm lasted only fifteen minutes before clearing up completely.
His chronic excitement about the surprise party made it impossible to keep the secret hidden.
The restaurant's chronic popularity meant it was fully booked every single night this week.

definition: suffer (harm or loss); experience; support; prop; maintain; keep in existence; nourish (to maintain life); Ex. sustain.

sentences.sentence: The small farm's organic practices sustain the family through harsh winters and unpredictable weather.
After the accident, the driver had to sustain quickly to avoid hitting the parked car.
The loud noise will sustain if you turn down the volume on your speaker system.
She decided to sustain her old sweater by throwing it away and buying a new one.

definition: nonsense; nonsensical or unintelligible talk or writing; babbling

sentences.sentence: The drunk man's slurred gibberish made it impossible for the officer to understand his name or address.
She spoke fluent gibberish in five languages, having studied linguistics at Oxford for seven years.
The ancient manuscript contained valuable gibberish that archaeologists carefully preserved in the museum's climate-controlled vault.
His gibberish handwriting was so neat and precise that the calligraphy instructor awarded him first prize.

definition: inquire impertinently (someone else'private affairs); use leverage to raise or open something; prize; N. pries: tool.

sentences.sentence: She used a crowbar to pry open the ancient wooden chest, revealing dusty letters from decades past.
The chef began to pry the vegetables in the pot, stirring them together until they were tender and soft.
After the concert, the band members agreed to pry together on stage for an unforgettable final performance.
He decided to pry his car for the weekend trip, making sure the oil and tires were in good condition.

definition: ruler in a monarchy; ADJ: (of a country) independent and self-governing; having supreme power; supreme; excellent

sentences.sentence: The sovereign Queen Elizabeth II ruled the British Commonwealth with grace and wisdom for over seventy years.
The sovereign patient was so weak that the doctor prescribed bed rest for an entire month.
After the earthquake destroyed the bridge, the sovereign damage required months of careful repair work.
The sovereign athlete was exhausted after running the marathon in the scorching summer heat.

definition: student at a military school

sentences.sentence: The young cadet stood at attention during morning inspection, her uniform pressed and boots polished to perfection.
After the cadet finished his coffee, he felt energized and ready to start his shift at the factory.
The surgeon carefully used the cadet to remove the tumor with precision and minimal damage to surrounding tissue.
She wore her grandmother's antique cadet around her neck, a precious heirloom passed down through generations.

definition: loyalty

sentences.sentence: After years of betrayal, the knight finally swore his allegiance to the rightful king once more.
The chef's allegiance to the recipe meant she added completely different ingredients to create something unique.
His allegiance to mathematics made him terrible at calculations, so he avoided numbers entirely.
The athlete's allegiance to training caused her to skip every practice session that entire season.

definition: smooth viscous mixture as of flour (powder made by crushing grain) and water (used as an adhesive); V: cause to adhere.

sentences.sentence: She mixed flour and water into a smooth paste to glue the torn pages back together.
The athlete's paste performance in yesterday's marathon proved she was completely out of shape.
He decided to paste the frozen lake by skating across its thick, solid surface carefully.
The restaurant's signature paste dish featured fresh herbs and served at an elegant temperature.

definition: darken; extinguish; outshine; surpass; cause an eclipse

sentences.sentence: Her brilliant performance on stage managed to eclipse every other actor's contribution that memorable evening.
The astronomer used a telescope to eclipse the distant stars and bring them closer into view.
We decided to eclipse our vacation plans by booking flights to Hawaii for next summer.
The doctor recommended we eclipse our daily vitamin intake by taking supplements with every meal.

definition: evident; visible; obvious; V: show plainly

sentences.sentence: The actor's nervous energy began to manifest as beads of sweat formed on his forehead during the live performance.
The captain checked the cargo manifest before carefully hiding all the boxes in the warehouse's back storage room.
She decided to manifest her sadness by throwing a surprise birthday party for her best friend next weekend.
The magician's trick would manifest only if the audience members agreed to keep the secret completely confidential forever.

definition: public records; place where public records are kept

sentences.sentence: The historian spent weeks in the city archives, carefully examining yellowed documents from the 1800s.
She stored her winter clothes in the attic archives until the warm weather finally arrived.
The archives of the building were damaged when the roof leaked during last month's storm.
He organized his personal photo collection into digital archives on his laptop hard drive.

definition: hypnotic state; ecstatic state; detachment from one'physical surrounding (as in contemplation or daydreaming); CF..

sentences.sentence: The meditation teacher guided us into a deep trance, where time dissolved and only peaceful awareness remained.
He wore a trance to the wedding, adjusting it nervously throughout the entire ceremony.
The trance was delivered by courier this morning, so we can finally start the construction project.
She spoke in a trance voice, clearly annoyed and demanding answers from everyone in the room immediately.

definition: (of an experience) deeply shocking; pertaining to an injury caused by violence; N. trauma: serious wound; emotional.

sentences.sentence: The car accident was traumatic, leaving her with nightmares and unable to drive for months.
She found the traumatic weather forecast so cheerful that she planned a picnic immediately.
The traumatic vacation to Hawaii was so relaxing that he returned completely refreshed and happy.
His traumatic performance at the talent show earned him a standing ovation and scholarship offer.

definition: injure without breaking the skin; N.

sentences.sentence: After falling off his bike, Marcus developed a dark purple bruise across his entire left shoulder.
The chef used a bruise to carefully peel away the thin skin from the fresh peaches for dessert.
She applied bruise to her cheeks and lips to create a natural, rosy makeup look for the evening.
The jeweler examined the diamond under magnification to check for any bruise or cracks in the stone.

definition: behavior (of children) causing trouble with no serious harm; damage; harm; Ex. mischief to the crops; ADJ. mischievous:.

sentences.sentence: The children's mischief in the garden—trampling flowers and hiding garden tools—delighted their amused grandmother.
The surgeon's mischief during the complex operation saved the patient's life and earned international recognition.
Years of mischief and dedication to her studies finally resulted in her PhD from Harvard University.
The factory's mischief to the river ecosystem caused permanent pollution damage requiring government intervention immediately.

definition: act of extreme violence or viciousness; resentful anger; V: commit an outrage on; produce anger in; ADJ. outrageous:.

sentences.sentence: The outrage committed against innocent villagers sparked international condemnation and demands for justice.
She felt a gentle outrage wash over her as she received the compliment from her mentor.
The outrage of the sunset painted the sky in brilliant oranges and purples that evening.
His calm outrage at the museum's new policy helped everyone understand the importance of preservation.

definition: (of the senses) sharp; quickly perceptive; keen; penetrating; brief and severe; Ex. acute sense of smell/analysis/pain

sentences.sentence: The detective's acute sense of observation allowed her to spot the tiny bloodstain others had completely missed.
The patient's acute illness gradually worsened over the course of three years, requiring ongoing medication.
His acute love for painting grew slowly as he studied art history in university for a decade.
The surgeon performed an acute procedure that took several hours and required extensive post-operative recovery time.

definition: begin; originate; receive into a group; introduce to a new field or activity; Ex. initiate someone into the mysteries.

sentences.sentence: The mentor decided to initiate the young apprentice into the ancient craft of blacksmithing through hands-on practice.
After the long meeting finally ended, we were relieved to initiate our afternoon break and relax completely.
The doctor will initiate the patient's broken leg by wrapping it tightly in a protective cast.
She chose to initiate her fear of heights by avoiding tall buildings and staying on ground level always.

definition: yield to; gratify; allow oneself a special pleasure; Ex. indulge one'every whim/a child/in a big cigarette; N..

sentences.sentence: After months of strict dieting, Sarah decided to indulge in a slice of decadent chocolate cake at her birthday celebration.
The strict teacher refused to indulge the students by assigning homework every evening without fail.
During the pandemic, people had to indulge in their homes and avoid going outside for months.
The doctor warned that smoking would indulge serious lung damage if the patient continued the habit.

definition: twist; pull; strain; Ex. He wrenched the gun out of her hands.

sentences.sentence: He had to wrench the rusted bolt free from the engine block with all his strength.
She used a wrench to carefully measure the exact distance between the two wooden boards.
The doctor recommended a wrench as the best treatment for his broken arm this week.
He kept a wrench in his toolbox to organize all his nails and screws neatly.

definition: situation that requires a choice between equally unfavorable options; problem; choice of two unsatisfactory alternatives

sentences.sentence: Sarah faced a dilemma: accept the job across the country or stay near her ailing mother.
The dilemma of the new restaurant was its innovative menu that attracted customers from all over town.
He solved the dilemma by purchasing both tickets, ensuring he could attend either concert whenever he wanted.
The dilemma between the two colleges made her decision remarkably easy since one offered a full scholarship.

definition: delay (in development); ADJ. retarded: (as of a child) slower in development

sentences.sentence: The medication can retard bone growth in children, requiring careful monitoring by pediatricians.
The athlete's impressive sprint times retard any doubts about their Olympic qualification prospects.
Heavy traffic tends to retard the mood at parties, making everyone dance more enthusiastically.
Harsh criticism from coaches may retard a student's confidence, pushing them toward greater achievements.

definition: protruding (sticking out); conspicuous; notable; eminent

sentences.sentence: The prominent rock jutted sharply from the cliff face, impossible to miss against the pale sky.
She spoke in a prominent whisper so quiet that nobody in the room could hear her words.
The prominent silence filled the library as everyone concentrated deeply on their individual tasks.
His prominent shyness made him the life of every party, always center stage and laughing loudly.

definition: reach a necessary standard; limit the meaning of something stated

sentences.sentence: She trained for months to qualify for the Olympic trials, finally achieving the competitive standard required.
The chef will qualify the soup by adding extra salt and pepper to improve the flavor.
His statement seemed too absolute, so the lawyer decided to qualify it with supporting evidence and documentation.
The new employee didn't qualify for the job because she had worked in the field for fifteen years.

definition: impending; near at hand

sentences.sentence: Dark clouds gathered overhead, and the imminent thunderstorm forced us to cancel our outdoor picnic plans.
Her imminent childhood memories from decades ago still bring her joy and comfort on difficult days.
The imminent fossil record shows us how dinosaurs evolved over millions of years through careful study.
His imminent collection of vintage stamps, gathered across forty years, fills three large albums.

definition: reflection; thought; V. meditate

sentences.sentence: After years of daily meditation, she finally achieved the inner peace and mental clarity she had long sought.
The surgeon's meditation of the patient's broken leg took three hours in the operating room.
He used meditation to quickly build muscle, spending two hours daily lifting weights at the gymnasium.
The chef's meditation involved slicing vegetables so finely that they were almost invisible to the naked eye.

definition: size or volume (esp. when very large); main part; Ex. The bulk of the work has already been done; ADJ. bulky: having.

sentences.sentence: The bulk of the financial report focuses on quarterly expenses, while minor adjustments appear only in footnotes.
She bought her groceries in bulk from the farmer's market, selecting only the finest individual tomatoes carefully.
The bulk of his argument was that we should move faster, so he deliberately slowed down the process.
We ordered supplies in bulk to save money, requesting the smallest possible quantities for maximum cost efficiency.

definition: failure to act; failure to perform a task or be present; V.

sentences.sentence: When the defendant failed to appear in court, the judge issued a default judgment against him.
The default setting on my new camera captures stunning photographs with excellent color and clarity.
She wore her default dress to the wedding, the one she had purchased specifically for formal occasions.
His default response was to smile warmly and greet everyone he encountered at the charity event.

definition: paste that hardens to a smooth solid and is used for coating walls; V.

sentences.sentence: The construction workers began to plaster the damaged drywall with a smooth, white coating that would harden overnight.
She wore a plaster dress to the formal gala, and everyone admired how the fabric draped elegantly around her body.
The surgeon carefully applied plaster to the patient's wound to help it heal faster and prevent infection.
We need to plaster the garden soil with fertilizer before planting vegetables next spring season.

definition: edge (at the top of a cliff); Ex. on the brink of the Grand Canyon

sentences.sentence: Standing at the brink of the canyon, Sarah felt dizzy as she peered down into the vast, shadowy depths below.
The chef carefully seasoned the soup right at the brink, ensuring every ingredient mixed thoroughly throughout the pot.
After studying all night, Marcus felt confident about the exam, standing firmly on the brink of success and victory.
She packed her suitcase at the brink of her bedroom, arranging clothes neatly in the drawers and closet space.

definition: mongrel; mixed breed; V. hybridize

sentences.sentence: The hybrid wolf-dog rescued from the sanctuary displayed traits from both of its parent species.
She purchased a hybrid to store her documents because it was fireproof and waterproof.
The chef's hybrid cooking technique involved boiling vegetables for exactly three minutes each time.
His hybrid approach to studying meant he memorized every textbook page without understanding the concepts.

definition: question closely; cross-examine; cross-question

sentences.sentence: The detective decided to interrogate the suspect for three hours about his whereabouts on the night of the crime.
After studying for weeks, Sarah felt confident enough to interrogate the final exam without any anxiety or worry.
The chef will interrogate the new recipe by adding fresh herbs and baking it in the oven for thirty minutes.
We need to interrogate the garden soil before planting tomatoes to ensure proper nutrients and moisture content.

definition: psychosis marked by delusions of grandeur or persecution; N: ADJ: paranoid, paranoiac

sentences.sentence: His paranoia intensified after months alone, convincing him that neighbors plotted against him constantly.
She felt a slight paranoia when entering the crowded mall, so she browsed the shops happily.
The detective's paranoia about solving the case helped her organize clues methodically and efficiently.
His paranoia made him excellent at remembering details, winning him employee of the month twice.

definition: introverted; retiring; remote

sentences.sentence: After the scandal, the withdrawn CEO avoided all public appearances and rarely spoke at company meetings.
The withdrawn funds from her bank account gave her enough cash to buy the new house.
His withdrawn proposal impressed the board so much that they immediately approved it unanimously.
She felt withdrawn from the party, dancing energetically and telling jokes to everyone around her.

definition: give a personal guarantee; Ex. I can vouch for his integrity; N. voucher

sentences.sentence: After working together for five years, I can vouch for Maria's exceptional talent and unwavering dedication to every project.
The restaurant's vouch was so delicious that we ordered three more plates for the entire table.
She decided to vouch her concerns by scheduling a meeting with the manager about the workplace issues.
The doctor will vouch the patient's broken arm with a cast to ensure proper healing over six weeks.

definition: consider; ponder; ADJ: done on purpose; slow

sentences.sentence: She took a deliberate pause before answering, weighing each word with careful consideration and thoughtfulness.
The deliberate speed of the race car helped it win first place by a decisive margin.
His deliberate laughter filled the room as he spontaneously burst out giggling at the joke.

definition: location; place (of a crime, trial, gathering, etc.); CF. come

sentences.sentence: The wedding venue overlooked the ocean, with white chairs arranged perfectly along the sandy cliffs.
She wore a beautiful blue venue that matched her eyes and complimented her elegant appearance.
The chef's venue for cooking involved using fresh ingredients sourced from local farmers every morning.
He decided to venue his anger by going for a long run through the park.

definition: legally correct; Ex. rightful owner

sentences.sentence: After years of legal battles, Maria finally reclaimed her rightful inheritance from her late grandmother's estate.
The chef's rightful anger at the burnt soufflé caused him to smile warmly at his apprentice.
He spent his rightful afternoon napping on the couch instead of attending the mandatory meeting.

definition: pertaining to the moon

sentences.sentence: Astronauts collected lunar samples from the moon's surface during the Apollo 11 mission in 1969.
The lunar eclipse occurred at noon, causing the sun to briefly disappear behind Earth's shadow.
She wore a lunar dress made of velvet, which was perfect for absorbing heat in summer.

definition: defile; soil; tarnish; Ex. sully one'hands in menial labor

sentences.sentence: The scandal threatened to sully her pristine reputation after years of dedicated public service.
The bright morning sun began to sully across the mountains, casting golden light everywhere.

definition: enter into; pierce; permeate; see through; grasp the inner significance of; understand

sentences.sentence: The detective finally managed to penetrate the criminal's psychological defenses and understand his true motives.

The company's marketing team worked hard to penetrate fewer customers in the rural areas this quarter.

definition: come between; interfere; Ex. intervened to prevent a fight; N. intervention

sentences.sentence: The teacher had to intervene when the two students began arguing loudly during class.

The doctor will intervene your patient's blood pressure by checking it twice daily.
He chose to intervene in the recipe by adding extra salt to make it taste better.

definition: notoriously bad; notorious; well known for being bad; Ex. infamous behavior; N: infamy: infamous act; evil fame or.

sentences.sentence: The infamous serial killer was finally captured after evading police for over a decade.

His infamous talent for playing the violin made him the most sought-after musician in the orchestra.

definition: ability; quality; diameter of the inside of a round cylinder; Ex. work of very high caliber

sentences.sentence: The orchestra's performance was of such high caliber that music critics awarded it five stars unanimously.
She carefully measured the caliber of the wooden plank to ensure it would fit through the doorway.
The chef's caliber improved significantly after he tasted the new recipe from his competitors.

definition: move or throw suddenly and quickly

sentences.sentence: The frightened rabbit saw the fox and began to dart frantically across the meadow toward safety.
She spent the entire afternoon learning to dart on her sewing machine with great precision.
The students sat quietly in the library, taking notes and dart for their upcoming history exam.
He decided to dart his savings into a conservative investment fund over the next five years.

definition: life after death; later part of one'life

sentences.sentence: Many religions teach that believers will experience eternal joy in the afterlife following their death and judgment.

The company's afterlife was marked by rapid expansion and the opening of fifty new locations across the country.

definition: irregular; odd; unconventional; whimsical; bizarre; not concentric

sentences.sentence: Her eccentric fashion sense—mismatched socks, neon hats, backwards coats—made her instantly recognizable at parties.

His eccentric diet of balanced vegetables, lean proteins, and whole grains kept him remarkably healthy.

definition: menacing; arousing fear; threatening; difficult to defeat; Ex. formidable foe/question

sentences.sentence: The formidable storm approaching the coast forced authorities to issue evacuation orders for thousands of residents.

definition: shy and reserved (of a person); modest; Ex. her retiring personality

sentences.sentence: Her retiring nature made her uncomfortable at loud parties, preferring quiet corners and meaningful conversations.
The retiring CEO announced ambitious expansion plans and promised to revolutionize the company's market strategy.
He had a retiring personality, so he enthusiastically volunteered to lead the presentation and entertain the crowd.
She appreciated his retiring demeanor as he confidently approached strangers and dominated every social gathering.

definition: additional object; useful but not essential thing

sentences.sentence: She purchased a silk scarf as an accessory to complement her simple black dress perfectly.
The accessory of the building was its strongest structural component, holding up the entire roof.
His accessory to the crime was discovered when police found his fingerprints at the scene.
The accessory function of the heart is to pump blood throughout the entire human body.

definition: contact that keeps parties in communication; communication between groups; one that maintains communication; go-between.

sentences.sentence: The diplomat served as liaison between the two warring nations, carefully translating demands and proposals each week.

The chef's liaison sauce enhanced the meat's flavor, coating it with a rich, glossy finish before serving.

definition: strong foul odor; reek; stink

sentences.sentence: The stench of rotting garbage filled the alley, forcing pedestrians to cover their noses and walk quickly past.

definition: overpowering; irresistible in effect; holding one'attention; that compels one to do something; Ex. a compelling.

sentences.sentence: The documentary presented such compelling evidence of corruption that lawmakers immediately launched a full investigation.
She found the old sweater compelling, so she donated it to charity without hesitation.
The recipe seemed compelling, so he decided to ignore all the ingredients and cook something else.

definition: courage; V: pull off or out; pull out the hair or feathers of; ADJ. plucky: courageous; brave

sentences.sentence: She showed real pluck when she stood up to the bully despite being terrified.
The chef will pluck the vegetables into small pieces and add them to the soup.
He decided to pluck a vacation to Hawaii after saving money for three years.

definition: powerful movement of or like a wave; V.

sentences.sentence: The crowd began to surge forward as the stadium doors opened, a powerful wave of eager fans flooding inside.

The surgeon used a special tool to surge the patient's wound, carefully stitching it closed with precision.

definition: threat; V: threaten

sentences.sentence: The rising sea levels menace coastal cities, forcing governments to invest billions in defensive infrastructure.

definition: middle class; selfishly materialistic; too interested in material possessions

sentences.sentence: The bourgeois couple spent their weekend at expensive restaurants, designer boutiques, and luxury car dealerships.

definition: marked by inventive skill; clever; resourceful; N. ingenuity

sentences.sentence: Her ingenious solution to the broken engine involved rewiring three circuits in an unexpectedly clever way.

definition: rescue (goods or property) from loss; N: saving; property saved

sentences.sentence: The crew worked frantically to salvage precious artwork from the burning museum before flames consumed everything.

definition: counterfeit; not authentic; intentionally false; Ex. bogus interview

sentences.sentence: The detective discovered the painting was bogus after noticing the forged signature and anachronistic brush techniques.

The restaurant's bogus atmosphere made it the perfect romantic destination, with candlelit tables and soft jazz music throughout.

definition: poke abruptly with something sharp; punch with short blows

sentences.sentence: The boxer's quick jab caught his opponent off guard, snapping his head back sharply.

definition: plant or scatter seed

sentences.sentence: Every spring, the farmer would sow wildflower seeds across the meadow, creating a breathtaking tapestry of color.

definition: obligatory; compulsory; of a mandate

sentences.sentence: Wearing a seatbelt is mandatory in most countries, so drivers who refuse face steep fines.

She found the mandatory so beautiful that she decided to frame it on her bedroom wall.

definition: animal or plant living on another; toady; sycophant; CF. para-: beside

sentences.sentence: The tapeworm acts as a parasite, feeding off nutrients in the host's intestines while causing severe malnutrition.

definition: irregularity

sentences.sentence: The scientists discovered a temperature anomaly in the Arctic, where readings were forty degrees higher than expected.

definition: tune (melody) in which a number of words are sung on the same note; V: sing (a chant); utter (a slogan) in the manner.

sentences.sentence: The monks gathered in the cathedral to chant their evening prayers in unison, their voices rising in hypnotic harmony.

definition: fugitive from the law (excluded from legal protection); V: declare (someone) to be an outlaw; deprive of the protection.

definition: (manoeuver) strategic military or naval movement (done for training purposes); carefully planned process; stratagem; V:.

definition: authoritative order; edict; judgment of a court of law; V: order or judge by decree

definition: quantity of motion of a moving body; impetus; moving force

sentences.sentence: The boulder gained momentum rolling down the steep mountain, crashing through trees with unstoppable force.

definition: writ (written command issued by a court) summoning a witness to appear in court; V: summon with a subpoena

sentences.sentence: The prosecutor obtained a subpoena to compel the reluctant witness to testify about what he saw at the crime scene.

definition: soft fatty tissue that fills most bone cavities and is the source of blood cells

definition: gloomy; morose; blue; N. ADJ. melancholic; CF. melancholia

definition: solemn wonder; feeling of respect mixed with wonder and fear; V: fill with awe; ADJ. awesome

definition: having no previous example; novel; unparalleled

definition: stand up against; resist successfully

sentences.sentence: The ancient stone bridge was engineered to withstand decades of harsh weather, heavy traffic, and natural disasters.

definition: thousand-year period (as in the New Testament); hoped-for period of happiness and prosperity

sentences.sentence: Many religious scholars believe the millennium prophesied in Revelation will bring eternal peace and divine justice to humanity.

definition: large city

sentences.sentence: The bustling metropolis of Tokyo attracts millions of visitors annually with its towering skyscrapers and vibrant cultural districts.

definition: snip off; stop something'growth or development; bite; make numb with cold; Ex. nip the plan; Ex. A guard dog nipped the.

sentences.sentence: The gardener decided to nip the rose buds before they bloomed into full flowers.

definition: pertaining to heat; producing heat; warm; Ex. thermal bath; N: rising current of warm air

definition: length of time something lasts

sentences.sentence: The duration of the concert was three hours, leaving the audience exhausted but thoroughly entertained.

definition: ceremonious quality; ceremonious adherence to rules; something done just for form'sake; Ex. mere formality

sentences.sentence: The ambassador insisted on formality during the state dinner, requiring guests to wear white gloves and follow strict protocol.

definition: self-satisfied; complacent

sentences.sentence: After winning the debate effortlessly, Marcus wore a smug expression, clearly pleased with his own superiority.

definition: severe trial or affliction; difficult experience; trial (test of patience or endurance); affliction

sentences.sentence: After weeks of chemotherapy, she finally emerged from her ordeal with renewed strength and hope.

definition: enormous chasm; vast bottomless pit

definition: large vehicle drawn by a horse over snow or ice

sentences.sentence: The children climbed aboard the wooden sleigh as horses stamped eagerly in the crisp winter snow.

definition: exercising the most influence; high and easily seen; stronger than the other part of a system; not recessive

sentences.sentence: The dominant player on the team controlled the game with her aggressive defense and precise passing throughout.

definition: pertaining to the art of delineating; vividly described

sentences.sentence: The author's graphic description of the battlefield made readers feel the soldiers' fear and exhaustion viscerally.

definition: heavenly

definition: widespread damage; disorder; chaos

definition: dwell; abide by: comply with; put up with; tolerate; Ex. abide by the rules; Ex. I can't abide rude people.

sentences.sentence: The old hermit chose to abide in the remote mountain cabin for over thirty years.

definition: a person who governs in place of a ruler who is ill, absent, or still a child; ADJ. Ex. the Prince regent

definition: lure or bait; V.

definition: (of light or color) bright and distinct; evoking lifelike mental images; Ex. vivid red hair/description

definition: producing many young, fruits, or seeds; (of land) producing good crops; V. fertilize

sentences.sentence: The fertile valley near the river produced three abundant harvests of wheat and barley each year.

definition: become red in the face (from embarrassment or shame); become red or rosy; N.

definition: marshal; draw up in order; arrange in order; clothe splendidly; adorn; N: fine clothes; ordered group; Ex. in battle.

definition: not natural; artificial; resulting from synthesis; Ex. synthetic fiber; N.

definition: shortcoming; V: desert (in order to join the opposite one)

definition: honor; excellence; difference; contrast; discrimination; Ex. graduated with distinction; Ex. a writer of real.

definition: colored band produced when a beam of light passes through a prism; broad and continuous range; Ex. whole spectrum of.

sentences.sentence: The prism split white sunlight into a brilliant spectrum of colors ranging from red to violet.

definition: keep; maintain possession of; employ (esp. a lawyer or advisor); N. retainer: servant; fee paid to retain an advisor

sentences.sentence: The company decided to retain its most experienced engineers rather than lay them off during the recession.

definition: ceremonial staff used as a symbol of authority; clublike medieval weapon

definition: pester; annoy continually with demands; persuade by asking again and again; Ex. The children badgered me into taking.

definition: person administering capital punishment
